The following are the indications of rain : Tasteless water,
the sky with the colour of cow's eyes or crow's eggs (white and
blue), the quarters being uncontaminated, salt becoming moist,
subdued wind, much fumbling of fishes ashore and the repeated
croakings of frogs.
[These symptoms are familiar even to rustics of India. The
metre of the verse is ^F^rfY.]

Immediate rain is indicated by cats scratching the ground
vehemently with their nails, accumulation of rust on iron or bronze
vessels with the smell of raw meat or fish, and construction of
bridges on the roads by children.
[In the third line f^f^rf^cTTH is another reading.
